method_description: Aggregate residential hot-water pathway using the current mix of electric, gas, liquid-fuel, and biomass water heating.
evidence_summary: Uses the aggregate residential building energy evidence and the water-heating boundary decision to isolate a hot-water service pathway.
derivation_method: Reduced-form split from aggregate residential building services using the v1 water-heating activity coefficient and incumbent fuel shares.
review_notes: Temporary aggregate pathway for comparison with explicit technology methods; replace with end-use evidence when available.

method_description: Aggregate transition pathway with strong electric heat-pump uptake and declining gas-boosted hot-water service.
evidence_summary: Australian electrification evidence supports heat-pump hot-water uptake as a core residential building transition measure.
derivation_method: Smoothed pathway coefficients reduce final-energy intensity and direct combustion while preserving one hot-water service unit.
review_notes: Represents an aggregate pathway, not a single heat-pump technology row.

method_description: Highly electrified residential hot-water pathway with negligible direct on-site combustion emissions.
evidence_summary: Heat-pump water heating and broader residential electrification evidence define the near-zero direct-emissions frontier.
derivation_method: Exploratory frontier pathway with electricity-only service delivery after transition years.
review_notes: Useful for pathway-vs-technology comparison; costs and uptake limits should be replaced by technology calibration later.

method_description: Gas storage or instantaneous water-heating technology serving useful residential hot-water demand.
evidence_summary: Gas water heating represents the fossil incumbent technology family inside the hot-water technology bundle.
derivation_method: Exploratory representative technology row using direct gas input intensity and NGA combustion factors.
review_notes: Incumbent share is a placeholder until simple-msm-techrep-1.4 calibrates a mixed 2025 technology stock.

method_description: Resistive electric storage or instantaneous water-heating technology.
evidence_summary: Resistive electric water heating remains a common direct-electric technology but is less efficient than heat pumps.
derivation_method: Representative direct-electric technology row with electricity input near one final-energy unit per service unit.
review_notes: Kept separate from heat pumps so technology-bundle solves can distinguish low-capex/high-energy electric service.

method_description: Electric heat-pump water-heating technology delivering hot-water service with lower final-energy input.
evidence_summary: Australian residential electrification evidence identifies heat-pump hot water as a core appliance replacement route.
derivation_method: Representative high-efficiency electric technology row with improving cost and input intensity over milestone years.
review_notes: Primary technology candidate for the later pathway-vs-technology comparison.

method_description: Solar-assisted hot-water technology with electric or gas boosting represented as residual final-energy input.
evidence_summary: Solar or boosted water heating is a locally relevant low-direct-emissions route where site conditions permit.
derivation_method: Representative boosted technology row; solar contribution is represented by lower purchased final-energy input rather than a separate commodity.
review_notes: Availability is capped because roof suitability, replacement timing, and boost configuration are heterogeneous.
